inline_comments.message:
Please don't hardcode the password

Instead of encrypting user passwords in json files you should log them in irreversible form (use hash instead of encryption)

This is legacy code from initial ATT seeding of DMaaP DR and I am apprehensive of removing it altogether.

I can hash the passwords in the logs but I can't be sure that ATT aren't still using the PasswordProcessor class for other use cases.

Hmm personally I'd say that as long as this is not a part of API (and it's not) and there is no usage of this class in the open source code, it should be removed. If anyone has some proprietary changes that are using this, they should be aware of the risk that it may be removed.

Otherwise we would end up in a situation that we cannot remove any single class form ONAP because some proprietary changes may be always using that class
